project.name: Pennies
project.description: Personal finance and expense tracking application — full-stack monorepo with React/TanStack frontend and .NET Clean Architecture microservices backend

nodes.name: routeTree.gen.ts
nodes.summary: Auto-generated TanStack Router route tree that registers and types all application routes, exporting the root routeTree used to initialize the router.
nodes.languageNotes: Auto-generated by TanStack Router's file-based routing. Encodes the full type-safe route hierarchy and should not be manually edited.

nodes.name: _authenticated.tsx
nodes.summary: TanStack Router layout route that guards all authenticated sections of the app, ensuring only verified users can access child routes.

nodes.name: dashboard.tsx
nodes.summary: Dashboard page route component that fetches and displays a summary of the user's expenses using a suspense query.

nodes.name: DashboardPage
nodes.summary: React component rendering the dashboard view; fetches expense data via useSuspenseQuery and renders it in a list.

nodes.name: expenses.tsx
nodes.summary: TanStack Router layout route acting as the parent segment for all expense-related nested routes under the authenticated area.

nodes.name: add.tsx
nodes.summary: Add-expense page route that renders a form for creating a new expense, then invalidates the expenses query and navigates back on save.

nodes.name: AddExpensePage
nodes.summary: React component providing the add-expense form; calls createExpenseFn on submit, invalidates queries, and navigates to the expenses list.

nodes.name: index.tsx
nodes.summary: Expenses list page that fetches all expenses, supports filter and sort via URL search params, and displays a toast notification on successful add.

nodes.name: ExpensesPage
nodes.summary: React component that renders the paginated, filterable expenses list with sorting controls and a transient toast notification on success.

nodes.name: check-email.tsx
nodes.summary: Post-registration check-email page that shows the user their pending verification email address and lets them resend the confirmation or use a different address.

nodes.name: CheckEmailPage
nodes.summary: React component displaying the check-email confirmation screen, handling resend and use-different-email actions.

nodes.name: sign-in.tsx
nodes.summary: Sign-in page route that renders an email/password login form with Google OAuth option and navigation links to sign-up and forgot-password flows.

nodes.name: SignInPage
nodes.summary: React component implementing the sign-in form, calling loginFn on submit and handling Google OAuth redirect.

nodes.name: sign-up.tsx
nodes.summary: Sign-up page route that renders a registration form and redirects to check-email on successful account creation.

nodes.name: SignUpPage
nodes.summary: React component implementing the registration form, calling registerFn on submit and navigating to the check-email route.

nodes.name: verified.tsx
nodes.summary: Email-verified confirmation page that informs the user their email has been verified and provides a continue button to the dashboard.

nodes.name: VerifiedPage
nodes.summary: React component shown after successful email verification, offering navigation to the main application.

nodes.name: verify-email.tsx
nodes.summary: Email verification callback page that reads a token from search params, calls verifyEmailFn, and navigates to the verified page on success.

nodes.name: VerifyEmailPage
nodes.summary: React component that automatically triggers email verification via token on mount and redirects to the verified route on success.

nodes.name: verify.tsx
nodes.summary: OTP/code verification page that reads search params to present the user with a verification step before proceeding to the authenticated area.

nodes.name: VerifyPage
nodes.summary: React component presenting a verification step (e.g., TOTP/OTP), navigating forward on success or back on cancellation.

nodes.name: index.tsx
nodes.summary: Root index route that redirects unauthenticated users to sign-in or authenticated users onward to the dashboard.

nodes.name: CheckEmail.tsx
nodes.summary: Desktop auth component displayed after sign-up, prompting the user to check their inbox, with callbacks for resending the confirmation email or switching to a different address.

nodes.name: DesktopCheckEmail
nodes.summary: Presentational React component rendering the check-email confirmation screen with resend and use-different-address action buttons, fully i18n-aware via react-i18next.

nodes.name: RegSuccess.tsx
nodes.summary: Desktop auth component shown after successful email verification, displaying the confirmed email address and offering a continue action.

nodes.name: DesktopRegSuccess
nodes.summary: Presentational React component rendering the registration success screen using CelebrateShell, showing the confirmed email and a continue button.

nodes.name: SignIn.tsx
nodes.summary: Desktop sign-in form component with email and password fields, Google OAuth button, forgot-password link, and sign-up navigation, wired via callback props.

nodes.name: DesktopSignIn
nodes.summary: Stateful React component managing email and password input state and delegating form submission and OAuth to parent-supplied callbacks; displays inline error messages.

nodes.name: SignUp.tsx
nodes.summary: Desktop sign-up form component with name, email, and password fields, Google OAuth button, and sign-in navigation, driven entirely by callback props.

nodes.name: DesktopSignUp
nodes.summary: Stateful React component that holds name, email, and password state locally and delegates submission and OAuth to parent callbacks; displays an inline error banner when present.

nodes.name: TwoFactor.tsx
nodes.summary: Desktop two-factor authentication component rendering a six-digit OTP input with auto-focus advancement and backspace navigation between digit boxes.

nodes.name: DesktopTwoFactor
nodes.summary: Stateful React component managing a six-element OTP code array with ref-based focus management; fires onVerify once all digits are entered and supports resend and back actions.

nodes.name: CheckEmail.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Vitest/React Testing Library unit tests for DesktopCheckEmail, verifying rendered text, resend callback invocation, and use-different-address callback.

nodes.name: RegSuccess.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Vitest/React Testing Library unit tests for DesktopRegSuccess, verifying that the success screen renders and the continue callback fires on button click.

nodes.name: SignIn.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Vitest/React Testing Library unit tests for DesktopSignIn, covering field rendering, user input, form submission, error display, Google OAuth, forgot-password, and sign-up navigation callbacks.

nodes.name: SignUp.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Vitest/React Testing Library unit tests for DesktopSignUp, verifying all input fields, form submission with collected data, error banner, and callback invocations.

nodes.name: TwoFactor.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Vitest/React Testing Library tests for DesktopTwoFactor, covering OTP digit input, auto-advance focus, backspace navigation, verify callback, resend, and back actions.

nodes.name: shared.tsx
nodes.summary: Shared UI primitives and layout shells for desktop auth screens, exporting DesktopField, DesktopPrimaryBtn, DesktopGoogleBtn, OrDivider, AuthShell, and CelebrateShell along with the internal BrandPanel.
nodes.languageNotes: BrandPanel is intentionally unexported and used only as a private implementation detail inside AuthShell; CelebrateShell contains its own inline brand panel duplicate for layout flexibility.

nodes.name: DesktopField
nodes.summary: Reusable labeled input field component with optional right-slot for actions such as a forgot-password link, used across all desktop auth forms.

nodes.name: DesktopPrimaryBtn
nodes.summary: Full-width primary action button with Lagoon brand color, used as the main submit control in desktop auth forms.

nodes.name: DesktopGoogleBtn
nodes.summary: Full-width Google OAuth button rendering the GoogleG logo alongside a configurable label, used in sign-in and sign-up flows.

nodes.name: OrDivider
nodes.summary: Decorative horizontal divider with an 'or' label, separating primary form submission from alternative OAuth entry points.

nodes.name: AuthShell
nodes.summary: Two-column layout shell used by sign-in and sign-up screens, rendering the branded left panel (BrandPanel) and a centered form panel on the right with title, subtitle, children, and optional footer.

nodes.name: CelebrateShell
nodes.summary: Two-column celebration layout shell used for post-action screens (e.g., RegSuccess, CheckEmail), showing a branded left panel and a centered glyph-icon panel with title and subtitle on the right.

nodes.name: CheckEmail.tsx
nodes.summary: Mobile-layout check-email confirmation screen shown after registration, prompting the user to verify their inbox with resend and use-different-email actions.

nodes.name: MobileCheckEmail
nodes.summary: Renders the mobile check-email screen with the user's email address, a resend button, and a use-different-email link.

nodes.name: RegSuccess.tsx
nodes.summary: Mobile-layout registration success screen displayed after email verification is confirmed, with a continue-to-app call-to-action.

nodes.name: MobileRegSuccess
nodes.summary: Renders the mobile registration success screen with a checkmark glyph, confirmed email display, and a Go to Pennies button.

nodes.name: SignIn.tsx
nodes.summary: Mobile-layout sign-in form component with email/password fields, Google OAuth button, and navigation links to sign-up and forgot-password flows.

nodes.name: MobileSignIn
nodes.summary: Stateful mobile sign-in form managing email and password inputs, invoking onSubmit, onGoogle, onSignUp, and onForgot callbacks.

nodes.name: SignUp.tsx
nodes.summary: Mobile-layout sign-up form component with full-name, email, and password fields plus a Google OAuth registration option.

nodes.name: MobileSignUp
nodes.summary: Stateful mobile sign-up form managing name, email, and password inputs, calling onSubmit with all three values and supporting Google registration.

nodes.name: TwoFactor.tsx
nodes.summary: Mobile-layout two-factor authentication screen with a 6-digit OTP input grid that auto-advances focus on entry and supports backspace navigation.

nodes.name: MobileTwoFactor
nodes.summary: Renders a 6-cell numeric OTP input that auto-focuses the next cell on digit entry, moves focus backward on Backspace, and exposes verify, resend, and back callbacks.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses useRef array to imperatively manage focus across the six OTP input elements.

nodes.name: CheckEmail.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Vitest/React Testing Library test suite for MobileCheckEmail verifying heading text, email display, and resend and use-different-email callbacks.

nodes.name: RegSuccess.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Vitest/React Testing Library test suite for MobileRegSuccess verifying success heading, CTA button text, onContinue callback, and email prop rendering.

nodes.name: SignIn.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Vitest/React Testing Library test suite for MobileSignIn covering heading rendering, input presence, and all four callback props (onSubmit, onGoogle, onSignUp, onForgot).

nodes.name: SignUp.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Vitest/React Testing Library test suite for MobileSignUp verifying heading, three input fields, onSubmit payload, Google button, and sign-in navigation link.

nodes.name: TwoFactor.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Vitest/React Testing Library test suite for MobileTwoFactor covering OTP input count, maxLength, focus-advance on digit entry, backspace navigation, and all three action callbacks.

nodes.name: shared.tsx
nodes.summary: Shared primitive UI building blocks for the mobile auth screens, exporting AuthHeader, AuthInput, PrimaryBtn, GoogleButton, OrDivider, GlyphCircle, and the base input CSS class.

nodes.name: AuthHeader
nodes.summary: Renders the Pennies brand header with a page title and optional subtitle for mobile auth screens.

nodes.name: AuthInput
nodes.summary: Labelled input field with optional right-slot (used for inline Forgot link) styled to the mobile auth design system.

nodes.name: PrimaryBtn
nodes.summary: Full-width lagoon-coloured primary call-to-action button used across all mobile auth forms.

nodes.name: GoogleButton
nodes.summary: Full-width Google OAuth button rendering the GoogleG logo alongside a configurable label.

nodes.name: OrDivider
nodes.summary: Horizontal divider with centred 'or' label used between primary and Google sign-in/sign-up actions.

nodes.name: GlyphCircle
nodes.summary: Circular lagoon-coloured container used to display inline SVG glyphs on the check-email and registration-success screens.

nodes.name: Pennies.Auth.Api.csproj
nodes.summary: MSBuild project file for the Auth microservice, wiring project references to Pennies.Auth.Application, Pennies.Messaging.RabbitMQ, and Pennies.ServiceDefaults, and pulling in NuGet packages for ASP.NET Identity, EF Core (PostgreSQL), MediatR, FluentValidation, JWT token handling, and Scalar/OpenAPI.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses centralized package version management via Directory.Packages.props; Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Design is marked PrivateAssets=all so it is build-time only and not included in published output.

nodes.name: appsettings.Development.json
nodes.summary: Development-environment logging override for the Auth API, setting the default log level to Information and suppressing verbose ASP.NET Core framework logs to Warning.

nodes.name: appsettings.json
nodes.summary: Base runtime configuration for the Auth API, supplying the PostgreSQL connection string for the auth database (pennies_auth), shared JWT issuer/audience/secret settings used to sign and verify tokens consumed by both microservices, and global logging defaults.
nodes.languageNotes: The Jwt:Secret placeholder must be replaced with a cryptographically strong value of at least 32 characters before deployment; both Auth.Api and Core.Api must share identical Jwt values for cross-service token validation to succeed.

nodes.name: Pennies.Core.Api.csproj
nodes.summary: MSBuild project file for the Core expense-tracking API, wiring project references to Pennies.Application, Pennies.Infrastructure, and Pennies.ServiceDefaults, and pulling in NuGet packages for JWT Bearer authentication, FluentValidation, and Scalar/OpenAPI.
nodes.languageNotes: Leaner than the Auth API csproj — omits EF Core Design and Identity packages because the Core API delegates persistence to Pennies.Infrastructure and uses DbUp SQL migrations rather than EF Core code-first migrations.

nodes.name: appsettings.Development.json
nodes.summary: Development-environment logging override for the Core API, setting the default log level to Information and suppressing verbose ASP.NET Core framework logs to Warning.

nodes.name: appsettings.json
nodes.summary: Base runtime configuration for the Core API, supplying the PostgreSQL connection string for the main pennies database and the JWT issuer/audience/secret values that must be identical to those in the Auth API so that tokens issued by Auth.Api are accepted by Core.Api.
nodes.languageNotes: The connection string targets the pennies database (not pennies_auth), keeping the two microservices on separate Postgres databases while sharing the same server instance in development.

nodes.name: .env
nodes.summary: Live environment file supplying PostgreSQL credentials (POSTGRES_USER and POSTGRES_PASSWORD) consumed by the Docker Compose service at startup.
nodes.languageNotes: Should be git-ignored in production; db/.env.example exists as the committed template.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Committed template showing the required environment variable names (POSTGRES_USER, POSTGRES_PASSWORD) for developers to copy into db/.env before starting the local database container.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose definition that provisions a standalone PostgreSQL 17 Alpine container (port 5432) with credentials from db/.env, a default pennies database, and a named volume (pennies_data) for persistent storage — used for local development independent of the full Aspire orchestration.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses restart: unless-stopped so the container survives host reboots; the named volume pennies_data persists data across container recreations.

nodes.name: frontend-architect.md
nodes.summary: Claude agent definition for the frontend-architect role, specifying its responsibilities for planning and implementing TanStack Start routes, components, forms, and data-fetching with full project conventions (path aliases, shadcn/ui, TanStack Query/Form/Store, Tailwind v4) and a persistent memory system for cross-conversation institutional knowledge.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses YAML frontmatter to configure the agent name, model, color, and memory scope, with the markdown body serving as the agent system prompt.

nodes.name: task-orchestrator.md
nodes.summary: Claude agent definition for the task-orchestrator role, which acts as a pure router: it interprets every incoming user request, maps it to the most appropriate specialist agent via the Agent tool, and reports results — never performing domain work itself; includes a persistent memory system for retaining routing heuristics across conversations.
nodes.languageNotes: The agent's routing decision tree covers all task types present in the Pennies web project (code generation, testing, docs, debugging, architecture, formatting) and explicitly references TanStack ecosystem conventions as implicit context for inference.

nodes.name: test-writer.md
nodes.summary: Claude agent definition for the test-writer role, providing detailed testing conventions for the Pennies web stack: Vitest APIs, React Testing Library query priorities, TanStack Query test utilities, TanStack Form/Zod validation testing, route loader testing, and file co-location rules.
nodes.languageNotes: Explicitly calls out Vitest over Jest, requires `#/` path aliases in test imports, and provides a reusable `createTestQueryClient()` pattern as an inline code snippet within the agent system prompt.

nodes.name: ui-design-advisor.md
nodes.summary: Claude agent definition for the ui-design-advisor role, covering UI/UX analysis, layout strategy, component hierarchy, design tokens, accessibility (WCAG 2.1/2.2), responsive design, and framework selection guidance — with a structured output format including ASCII wireframes and an accessibility checklist.
nodes.languageNotes: Agent is framework-agnostic by design (mentions Material UI, Tailwind, Ant Design, Chakra UI, shadcn/ui, React, Vue, Angular) while the project's actual stack is Tailwind v4 + shadcn/ui.

nodes.name: .cta.json
nodes.summary: TanStack Create App scaffold configuration recording the choices made at project creation: file-router mode, Bun package manager, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn, ESLint, TanStack Form, TanStack Store, and TanStack Query add-ons.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Environment variable template defining the three external service URLs required at runtime: the core API, the auth API, and the frontend application URL.

nodes.name: .mcp.json
nodes.summary: Model Context Protocol server configuration registering the TalkToFigma MCP server (via bunx) for design-to-code integration within Claude Code in the web project.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Primary Claude Code guidance document for the web frontend, describing all dev commands, the mandatory task-orchestrator agent workflow, architecture overview (routing, SSR data fetching, path aliases, auth, UI components, server functions), and backend service endpoint contracts.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Developer getting-started guide for the Pennies TanStack Start frontend, covering dev/build/test commands, Tailwind CSS setup, ESLint/Prettier usage, shadcn component installation, file-based routing patterns, server functions, API routes, and data fetching via loaders and TanStack Query.

nodes.name: components.json
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui configuration specifying the new-york style, zinc base color, CSS variables, Tailwind v4 global stylesheet path, and `#/` path aliases for component, utils, ui, lib, and hooks directories.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Root package manifest for the Pennies web app defining the `#/*` → `src/*` import alias, dev/build/test/lint/format scripts, and all runtime and dev dependencies including the full TanStack ecosystem, shadcn/ui dependencies, React 19, Tailwind v4, i18next, Zod, Vitest, and Testing Library.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Node `imports` field for the `#/*` alias (native package-level import maps), complemented by `tsconfig.json` `paths` for TypeScript resolution. The pnpm `onlyBuiltDependencies` block restricts native builds to esbuild and lightningcss.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the Pennies web frontend enabling strict mode, ES2022 target, bundler module resolution, and dual `#/*` / `@/*` path aliases mapping to `src/*`.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `moduleResolution: bundler` (Vite-aware) with `allowImportingTsExtensions` and `noEmit`, meaning TypeScript is used solely for type-checking while Vite handles transpilation.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Exclusion patterns configuration for the understand-anything analysis tool, using .gitignore syntax to specify files and directories to skip during codebase scanning.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Developer guide for Claude Code covering build and run commands, Clean Architecture + CQRS project structure, key patterns (Result<T>/Error, MediatR pipeline, JWT auth flow, migration strategies), and package management conventions for the Pennies .NET backend.

nodes.name: Directory.Build.props
nodes.summary: Solution-wide MSBuild properties file that enforces net10.0 target framework, nullable reference types, and implicit usings across all projects in the solution.
nodes.languageNotes: Directory.Build.props is automatically imported by all .csproj files in the directory tree, making these settings apply solution-wide without repetition.

nodes.name: Directory.Packages.props
nodes.summary: Central Package Management configuration declaring all NuGet package versions for Aspire, messaging (MassTransit/RabbitMQ), application (MediatR/FluentValidation), infrastructure (EF Core/Npgsql), API (JWT Bearer/OpenAPI), and test packages.
nodes.languageNotes: Central Package Management (ManagePackageVersionsCentrally=true) ensures all projects share the same package versions; individual .csproj files include packages without specifying versions.

nodes.name: Pennies.slnx
nodes.summary: Visual Studio solution file in the new .slnx XML format, organizing 13 source projects (including AppHost, domain, application, infrastructure, API, auth, messaging, worker services) and 3 test projects.

nodes.name: appsettings.Development.json
nodes.summary: Development-environment application settings for the AppHost Aspire orchestrator, currently containing only an empty Parameters section for local configuration overrides.

nodes.name: Pennies.AppHost.csproj
nodes.summary: Project configuration for the .NET Aspire AppHost orchestrator, referencing Aspire hosting packages for PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, Mailpit, and JavaScript, with project references to all microservice and migration projects.

nodes.name: Program.cs
nodes.summary: NET Aspire orchestrator entry point that wires up all infrastructure (PostgreSQL with pgAdmin, RabbitMQ with management UI, Mailpit) and microservices (core API, auth API, worker, migrations, Vite frontend) with their dependencies and startup ordering.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses .NET Aspire's DistributedApplication builder pattern; WithExplicitStart() on migration projects ensures they run on demand before the APIs, while WaitFor() enforces startup ordering for APIs.

nodes.name: AssemblyMarker.cs
nodes.summary: Empty marker class used as a type anchor for assembly scanning, enabling MediatR and FluentValidation to auto-discover handlers and validators from the Application assembly via typeof(AssemblyMarker).Assembly.

nodes.name: LoggingBehavior.cs
nodes.summary: MediatR pipeline behavior that logs the request type name before and after dispatching to the next handler, providing lightweight request/response tracing for all CQRS operations.

nodes.name: LoggingBehavior
nodes.summary: Generic MediatR IPipelineBehavior that injects ILogger and wraps each request dispatch with informational log messages.

nodes.name: ValidationBehavior.cs
nodes.summary: MediatR pipeline behavior that runs all FluentValidation validators registered for a request type and throws ValidationException with aggregated failures before the handler executes, enforcing input validation across all CQRS operations.

nodes.name: ValidationBehavior
nodes.summary: Generic MediatR IPipelineBehavior that collects all IValidator<TRequest> instances, runs them synchronously, and throws ValidationException with all failures if any are found.

nodes.name: Error.cs
nodes.summary: Sealed record representing a typed application error with Code and Message fields, plus static factory methods for common error cases (NotFound, Conflict, Unauthorized, Validation) used in the railway-oriented Result<T> pattern.

nodes.name: Result.cs
nodes.summary: Railway-oriented Result<T> type with Success/Failure factory methods and IsSuccess/IsFailure/Value/Error properties, plus a non-generic static Result helper class for type-inferred factory calls used by all CQRS handlers.
nodes.languageNotes: The non-generic static Result class enables callers to write Result.Success(value) with type inference rather than Result<T>.Success(value), a common C# ergonomics pattern.

nodes.name: Result
nodes.summary: Generic Result<T> type encapsulating either a success value or an Error, with private constructors enforcing creation only through Success/Failure factory methods.

nodes.name: CreateExpenseCommand.cs
nodes.summary: MediatR command record for creating a new expense, carrying UserId, Title, optional Description, Amount, ExpenseCategory, and Date, returning Result<ExpenseResponse>.

nodes.name: CreateExpenseHandler.cs
nodes.summary: MediatR handler for CreateExpenseCommand that constructs a domain Expense entity from the command, persists it via the repository, and returns Result.Success with an ExpenseResponse DTO.

nodes.name: CreateExpenseHandler
nodes.summary: IRequestHandler implementation that persists a new Expense entity and returns the mapped ExpenseResponse wrapped in a success Result.

nodes.name: CreateExpenseValidator.cs
nodes.summary: FluentValidation validator for CreateExpenseCommand enforcing non-empty Title (max 200 chars), non-zero negative Amount, Date not in the future, valid ExpenseCategory enum value, and non-empty UserId.

nodes.name: CreateExpenseValidator
nodes.summary: AbstractValidator<CreateExpenseCommand> with rules for all required fields including amount sign, maximum length, date boundary, and category enum validation.

nodes.name: DeleteExpenseCommand.cs
nodes.summary: MediatR command record for deleting an expense by its ID, carrying ExpenseId and UserId for ownership verification, returning Result<bool>.

nodes.name: DeleteExpenseHandler.cs
nodes.summary: MediatR handler for DeleteExpenseCommand that fetches the expense, validates existence and ownership (returning NotFound or Unauthorized errors), then deletes it and returns Result.Success(true).

nodes.name: DeleteExpenseHandler
nodes.summary: IRequestHandler implementation that enforces ownership-checked deletion using the repository and returns typed error Results for not-found and unauthorized cases.

nodes.name: DeleteExpenseValidator.cs
nodes.summary: FluentValidation validator for DeleteExpenseCommand ensuring both ExpenseId and UserId are non-empty GUIDs.

nodes.name: ExpenseResponse.cs
nodes.summary: DTO record for expense API responses containing Id, Title, Description, Amount, Category (as integer), Date, and CreatedAt, co-located with an internal ExpenseMappings extension class providing the ToResponse() mapping method.

nodes.name: ExpenseMappings
nodes.summary: Internal static class providing the ToResponse() extension method that maps a domain Expense entity to an ExpenseResponse DTO, serializing ExpenseCategory as an integer.

nodes.name: GetExpenseByIdHandler.cs
nodes.summary: MediatR handler for GetExpenseByIdQuery that fetches a single expense by ID, validates existence and ownership, and returns Result.Success with the mapped ExpenseResponse DTO.

nodes.name: GetExpenseByIdHandler
nodes.summary: IRequestHandler implementation for GetExpenseByIdQuery that checks both entity existence and user ownership before returning a mapped response.

nodes.name: GetExpenseByIdQuery.cs
nodes.summary: MediatR query record for fetching a single expense by ID with UserId for ownership scoping, returning Result<ExpenseResponse>.

nodes.name: GetExpensesHandler.cs
nodes.summary: MediatR handler for GetExpensesQuery that retrieves all expenses for a user from the repository and returns a read-only list of mapped ExpenseResponse DTOs wrapped in Result.Success.

nodes.name: GetExpensesHandler
nodes.summary: IRequestHandler implementation for GetExpensesQuery that returns a read-only list of all user-scoped expenses as mapped DTOs.

nodes.name: GetExpensesQuery.cs
nodes.summary: MediatR query record for fetching all expenses belonging to a user, scoped by UserId, returning Result<IReadOnlyList<ExpenseResponse>>.

nodes.name: Pennies.Application.csproj
nodes.summary: MSBuild project file for the shared Application layer, referencing Pennies.Domain and pulling in FluentValidation and MediatR NuGet packages used by all CQRS handlers.

nodes.name: AuthEndpoints.cs
nodes.summary: Defines and maps all authentication Minimal API endpoints (register, login, verify-email, resend-confirmation, check-email) under the /auth route group, dispatching each request to a MediatR command or query.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses C# static extension method pattern to attach endpoint mapping to IEndpointRouteBuilder, keeping routing logic decoupled from Program.cs.

nodes.name: AuthEndpoints
nodes.summary: Static class providing the MapAuthEndpoints extension method that registers all five auth routes and delegates request handling to private async methods via MediatR ISender.

nodes.name: MapAuthEndpoints
nodes.summary: Extension method that registers the five authentication routes (POST register/login/verify-email/resend-confirmation, GET check-email) under the /auth route group.

nodes.name: ResultExtensions.cs
nodes.summary: Provides the ToHttpResult extension method that translates a Result<T> discriminated union into the appropriate ASP.NET Core IResult HTTP response (200/201 on success, 400/401/404/409 on failure).
nodes.languageNotes: Uses C# switch expressions on both the success status code and the error code string to map domain errors to HTTP semantics.

nodes.name: ToHttpResult
nodes.summary: Maps a Result<T> to an IResult HTTP response, returning 201/200 on success and 404/409/401/400 based on the error code string.

nodes.name: ExceptionHandlingMiddleware.cs
nodes.summary: ASP.NET Core middleware that catches FluentValidation.ValidationException (returning HTTP 422 with per-field error details) and any other unhandled exception (returning HTTP 500).

nodes.name: ExceptionHandlingMiddleware
nodes.summary: Primary constructor middleware that intercepts validation and unexpected exceptions from the request pipeline and serializes them to structured JSON error responses.

nodes.name: Pennies.Auth.Api.http
nodes.summary: Visual Studio HTTP scratch file pre-configured with the auth service base URL (http://localhost:5080) for manual endpoint testing during development.

nodes.name: Program.cs
nodes.summary: Entry point for the Pennies.Auth.Api microservice; bootstraps ASP.NET Identity with EF Core and Postgres, registers MediatR with logging/validation pipeline behaviors, wires DI for JWT and email confirmation token services, and maps all auth endpoints.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses .NET Aspire service defaults (AddServiceDefaults) and Scalar for OpenAPI UI in development.

nodes.name: launchSettings.json
nodes.summary: ASP.NET Core launch profiles for the Auth API, defining HTTP (port 5200) and HTTPS (ports 7153/5200) run configurations with ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT set to Development.

nodes.name: EmailConfirmationTokenService.cs
nodes.summary: Implements IEmailConfirmationTokenService using ASP.NET Core Data Protection to create and unpack tamper-proof email confirmation tokens that bundle a userId and an ASP.NET Identity token.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses IDataProtectionProvider with a named purpose string 'EmailConfirmation' to isolate the protector scope, preventing token reuse across different protection purposes.

nodes.name: EmailConfirmationTokenService
nodes.summary: Concrete implementation that wraps IDataProtector to protect (encode) and unprotect (decode) userId|identityToken composite payloads for email confirmation links.

nodes.name: AssemblyMarker.cs
nodes.summary: Empty marker class used to anchor typeof() calls for MediatR handler and FluentValidation scanner assembly registration in Program.cs.

nodes.name: LoginCommand.cs
nodes.summary: MediatR command record carrying email and password credentials, requesting a Result<LoginResponse> from the CQRS pipeline.

nodes.name: LoginHandler.cs
nodes.summary: MediatR handler for LoginCommand that validates user credentials via ASP.NET Identity, generates a JWT access token, and returns it wrapped in a Result<LoginResponse>.

nodes.name: LoginHandler
nodes.summary: Handles login by looking up the user by email, verifying the password with SignInManager, and issuing a JWT token with a 1-hour expiry on success.

nodes.name: LoginValidator.cs
nodes.summary: FluentValidation validator for LoginCommand enforcing a non-empty valid email address and a non-empty password.

nodes.name: RegisterCommand.cs
nodes.summary: MediatR command record for user registration carrying display name, email, password, and a confirmation base URL for the verification link.

nodes.name: RegisterHandler.cs
nodes.summary: MediatR handler for RegisterCommand that creates a new ASP.NET Identity user, generates an email confirmation token, packages it into a protected link, and publishes a SendConfirmationEmailEvent via the message broker.

nodes.name: RegisterHandler
nodes.summary: Coordinates user creation with UserManager, protects the identity token into a URL-safe confirmation link, and publishes an email event to RabbitMQ via IMessagePublisher.

nodes.name: RegisterValidator.cs
nodes.summary: FluentValidation validator for RegisterCommand enforcing non-empty display name, valid email, minimum-8-character password, and non-empty confirmation URL.

nodes.name: ResendConfirmationCommand.cs
nodes.summary: MediatR command record for resending the email confirmation link, carrying the user's email and the confirmation base URL.

nodes.name: ResendConfirmationHandler.cs
nodes.summary: MediatR handler that re-generates and re-publishes the email confirmation link for an unconfirmed user, guarding against non-existent or already-confirmed users.

nodes.name: ResendConfirmationHandler
nodes.summary: Looks up the user, checks email confirmation state, regenerates a protected confirmation token link, and publishes a new SendConfirmationEmailEvent.

nodes.name: ResendConfirmationValidator.cs
nodes.summary: FluentValidation validator for ResendConfirmationCommand requiring a non-empty valid email and a non-empty confirmation base URL.

nodes.name: VerifyEmailCommand.cs
nodes.summary: MediatR command record for email verification carrying the opaque confirmation token string.

nodes.name: VerifyEmailHandler.cs
nodes.summary: MediatR handler that unpacks the protected confirmation token, looks up the user by ID, and confirms the email with ASP.NET Identity, returning validation errors for cryptographically invalid or expired tokens.

nodes.name: VerifyEmailHandler
nodes.summary: Unprotects the composite token via IEmailConfirmationTokenService, finds the user, and calls UserManager.ConfirmEmailAsync; maps CryptographicException to a validation failure Result.

nodes.name: VerifyEmailValidator.cs
nodes.summary: FluentValidation validator for VerifyEmailCommand requiring a non-empty token string.

nodes.name: CheckEmailResponse.cs
nodes.summary: Immutable DTO record that returns whether a given email address is already registered (Exists flag).

nodes.name: LoginResponse.cs
nodes.summary: Immutable DTO record returned on successful login, containing the JWT access token string and its expiry timestamp.

nodes.name: RegisterResponse.cs
nodes.summary: Immutable DTO record returned on successful registration, carrying the new user's ID, email, and display name.

nodes.name: CheckEmailHandler.cs
nodes.summary: MediatR query handler that checks whether an email address is already registered by querying UserManager, returning a CheckEmailResponse with an Exists boolean.

nodes.name: CheckEmailHandler
nodes.summary: Resolves CheckEmailQuery by calling UserManager.FindByEmailAsync and wrapping the null-check result in a successful Result<CheckEmailResponse>.

nodes.name: CheckEmailQuery.cs
nodes.summary: Defines the MediatR query record for checking whether an email address is already registered, returning a Result-wrapped CheckEmailResponse.

nodes.name: LoggingBehavior.cs
nodes.summary: MediatR pipeline behavior that logs the start and completion of every request by name using ILogger.

nodes.name: LoggingBehavior
nodes.summary: Generic MediatR IPipelineBehavior that logs request entry and exit for all IRequest types in the Auth Application layer.

nodes.name: ValidationBehavior.cs
nodes.summary: MediatR pipeline behavior that runs all registered FluentValidation validators for a request and throws a ValidationException if any failures are found.

nodes.name: ValidationBehavior
nodes.summary: Generic MediatR IPipelineBehavior that aggregates FluentValidation failures and throws a ValidationException before reaching the handler.

nodes.name: Error.cs
nodes.summary: Defines the Error value-object record for the Auth Application layer, providing named factory methods for NotFound, Conflict, Unauthorized, and Validation error codes.

nodes.name: Result.cs
nodes.summary: Defines the generic Result<T> discriminated union used throughout the Auth Application layer to represent either a successful value or an Error without throwing exceptions.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses private constructors and static factory methods to enforce correct construction of the discriminated union.

nodes.name: AuthUser.cs
nodes.summary: Extends ASP.NET Core Identity's IdentityUser with a DisplayName and CreatedAt timestamp to form the Auth microservice's primary user entity.

nodes.name: AuthUser
nodes.summary: ASP.NET Identity user entity extended with DisplayName and CreatedAt for the Pennies auth system.

nodes.name: AuthDbContext.cs

nodes.name: IEmailConfirmationTokenService.cs
nodes.summary: Service interface for protecting and unprotecting email confirmation tokens, encapsulating the token round-trip between user ID, identity token, and opaque string.

nodes.name: JwtTokenService.cs

nodes.summary: Default connection string configuration for the Core migrations console app, pointing to the local pennies Postgres database.

nodes.summary: Returns the ISO date string of the first day of the current month.

nodes.name: formatVnd
nodes.summary: Formats a numeric amount as a Vietnamese Dong (VND) currency string with thousands separators and sign prefix.

nodes.name: themeStore.ts
nodes.summary: TanStack Store-backed theme state module providing setTheme, applyThemeClass, and a useTheme React hook for reactive theme switching with localStorage persistence.

nodes.name: useTheme
nodes.summary: React hook returning the current theme value from the TanStack Store, triggering re-renders on theme changes.

nodes.name: utils.ts
nodes.summary: Provides the cn utility function combining clsx and tailwind-merge for conditional and conflict-free Tailwind class composition.

nodes.name: cn
nodes.summary: Merges Tailwind CSS class strings using clsx for conditional logic and tailwind-merge to eliminate duplicate/conflicting classes.

nodes.name: router.tsx
nodes.summary: Instantiates the TanStack Router with the generated route tree, QueryClient context, and SSR query integration.

nodes.name: __root.tsx
nodes.summary: Root layout route providing the HTML document shell, theme initialization from localStorage, and TanStack Query DevTools mounting.

nodes.name: RootDocument
nodes.summary: Root document component that restores the theme from localStorage on mount, listens for system color scheme changes, and wraps app children in the HTML shell.

nodes.name: styles.css
nodes.summary: Global CSS file defining Tailwind v4 theme variables (colors, radii, fonts), dark mode overrides, and custom animation keyframes for the Pennies design system.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Tailwind CSS v4 @theme directive for CSS custom property-based design tokens rather than a tailwind.config.js file.

nodes.name: i18n-wrapper.tsx
nodes.summary: Test utility wrapper component providing the i18next I18nextProvider context for rendering i18n-dependent components in unit tests.

nodes.name: setup.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest global test setup file configuring jest-dom matchers and any global mocks needed for the test suite.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite build configuration for the frontend, registering the TanStack Router plugin and React plugin for development and production builds.

nodes.name: vitest.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest test runner configuration extending the Vite config, setting the jsdom environment and pointing to the global test setup file.

layers.name: Frontend Routes
layers.description: TanStack Router file-based route components handling page rendering and navigation guards for dashboard, expense management, and authentication flows.

layers.name: Frontend UI Components
layers.description: React UI components including app-specific desktop and mobile layouts, shadcn/ui primitives, global styles, and TanStack Query provider integrations.

layers.name: Frontend Library
layers.description: Frontend utility modules including API client functions for expenses and auth, Zustand theme store, i18n locale definitions, and shared helper utilities.

layers.name: Frontend Tests
layers.description: Vitest and React Testing Library test suites covering UI components, library utilities, i18n, and theme store behavior in the React frontend.

layers.name: API Endpoints
layers.description: ASP.NET Core Minimal API endpoint definitions, exception-handling middleware, and HTTP result extension helpers for the Core expense API and Auth microservice.

layers.name: Domain Layer
layers.description: Pure .NET domain model defining the Expense entity, ExpenseCategory enum, repository interface, and shared base types with no external dependencies.

layers.name: Application Layer
layers.description: MediatR CQRS commands, queries, handlers, FluentValidation validators, DTOs, and pipeline behaviors implementing expense management and authentication business logic.

layers.name: Infrastructure Layer
layers.description: EF Core DbContexts, repository implementations, database migration runners, the .NET Aspire AppHost orchestrator, and shared Aspire service defaults for OpenTelemetry and health checks.

layers.name: Messaging Layer
layers.description: Message contracts, MassTransit/RabbitMQ publisher implementation, and background Worker consumers for expense-created events and email confirmation delivery.

layers.name: Project Support
layers.description: Backend xUnit test suites, solution-level MSBuild configuration, PostgreSQL Docker Compose setup, and project tooling and documentation files spanning the full monorepo.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: The web/README.md is the best first stop for any newcomer: it describes Pennies as a personal finance and expense tracking monorepo, explains the two main workspaces (web/ for the React frontend and api/ for the .NET backend), and lists the dev commands needed to get running. Reading it first gives you the mental model that every subsequent step will fill in.

tour.title: Backend Orchestrator
tour.description: api/src/Pennies.AppHost/Program.cs is the .NET Aspire orchestrator that boots the entire backend in a single command. It wires up a PostgreSQL database with pgAdmin, a RabbitMQ broker, and both microservices (Auth and Core), injecting connection strings automatically so each service can find the others. Understanding this file first shows you which services exist and how they are composed before you look at any individual service.
tour.languageLesson: .NET Aspire AppHost projects use a distributed application builder pattern: AddProject<T>() registers a service, WithReference() injects a named resource's connection string into that service at runtime. This replaces manual docker-compose environment variable wiring.

tour.title: Frontend Entry Point & Router
tour.description: web/src/routes/__root.tsx is the HTML document shell and root layout of the TanStack Start app. It initialises the theme from localStorage, renders the TanStack Query DevTools, and provides the Outlet where every page component mounts. The companion web/src/router.tsx constructs the router with the generated route tree and the shared QueryClient, and web/src/routeTree.gen.ts is the auto-generated type-safe route registry — you never edit it manually, but it shows every route that exists.
tour.languageLesson: TanStack Router uses file-based routing: the directory structure under web/src/routes/ maps directly to URL paths, and routeTree.gen.ts is regenerated on every code change. This gives full TypeScript type-safety for route params and search params without any manual route registration.

tour.title: Auth Flow: Routes & Guard
tour.description: web/src/routes/_authenticated.tsx is a layout route that enforces the authentication boundary — unauthenticated visitors are redirected to sign-in before they can see any nested page. web/src/routes/index.tsx is the root redirect that decides where to send each visitor on first load. The auth pages under web/src/routes/auth/ (sign-in, sign-up, verify-email) form the complete registration and login flow that feeds into this guard.

tour.title: Frontend API Client & Auth Library
tour.description: web/src/lib/auth.ts contains every function the frontend calls against the Auth microservice: login, logout, register, verify-email, resend-confirmation. web/src/lib/expenses.ts does the same for the Core microservice — it fetches and creates expenses, and maps the backend integer category enum back to a string the UI can display. web/src/lib/constants.ts centralises all API base URLs and route path constants so they are defined once and reused everywhere.

tour.title: Core Domain Model
tour.description: api/src/Pennies.Domain/ is the innermost layer of the Clean Architecture and has zero external dependencies. Entity.cs provides the abstract base with Id, CreatedAt, and UpdatedAt. Expense.cs is the central domain entity, carrying title, amount, category, date, and the userId that scopes it to an owner. IExpenseRepository.cs declares the repository contract that the Application layer depends on, keeping all persistence details out of the domain.
tour.languageLesson: Clean Architecture's Dependency Rule states that inner layers must not reference outer ones. Pennies.Domain.csproj has no project or NuGet references at all — the domain is pure C# value objects and interfaces, making it trivially testable and framework-independent.

tour.title: CQRS Application Layer
tour.description: api/src/Pennies.Application/ sits above the Domain and implements all use-cases as MediatR commands and queries. Result.cs and Error.cs form a railway-oriented programming pair: every handler returns Result<T> instead of throwing, which forces the caller to explicitly handle failure. The CreateExpense command group (command record, handler, validator) illustrates the full CQRS pattern: the command carries data, the validator enforces invariants via FluentValidation, and the handler invokes IExpenseRepository to persist the new entity.
tour.languageLesson: MediatR pipeline behaviors intercept every request before it reaches its handler. ValidationBehavior.cs runs all FluentValidation validators registered for a request type and throws ValidationException on failure. LoggingBehavior.cs wraps the call in structured log entries. This cross-cutting concern is configured once in Program.cs and applies to every command and query automatically.

tour.title: Auth Microservice
tour.description: api/src/Pennies.Auth.Api/Program.cs bootstraps the Auth microservice: it registers ASP.NET Identity with EF Core, sets up JWT bearer auth, and mounts the Minimal API endpoints. AuthEndpoints.cs maps the six auth routes (register, login, verify-email, resend-confirmation, check-email) each dispatching a MediatR command. JwtTokenService.cs mints signed JWT tokens on successful login, and EmailConfirmationTokenService.cs uses ASP.NET Core Data Protection to create tamper-proof email verification tokens.

tour.title: Core Expense API
tour.description: api/src/Pennies.Core.Api/Program.cs starts the expense-tracking microservice, wiring MediatR, FluentValidation, infrastructure DI, and JWT validation. ExpenseEndpoints.cs maps the four expense CRUD routes — GET list, GET by id, POST create, DELETE — each extracting the UserId from the JWT claim and dispatching the appropriate MediatR query or command. ResultExtensions.cs converts Result<T> into the correct HTTP status code so the controller layer stays thin.

tour.title: Infrastructure & Database
tour.description: api/src/Pennies.Infrastructure/ provides the concrete EF Core implementation of IExpenseRepository: AppDbContext.cs exposes the Expenses DbSet, ExpenseConfiguration.cs maps column constraints, and ExpenseRepository.cs implements all CRUD operations against Postgres. The database schema itself is defined in the DbUp SQL migration script, which creates the Expenses table with UUID primary key, user ownership, and the integer category column that the domain enum maps to.
tour.languageLesson: Pennies uses two different migration strategies: DbUp (SQL script files) for the Core database and EF Core Migrations (C# code-first) for the Auth database. DbUp scripts are idempotent — they are tracked in a schema_versions table so re-runs are safe. EF Core migrations are ordered by timestamp prefix and applied automatically at startup.

tour.title: Async Messaging Layer
tour.description: When an expense is created, the handler publishes an ExpenseCreatedEvent via IMessagePublisher, and when a user registers, a SendConfirmationEmailEvent is published. These interfaces are defined in api/src/Pennies.Messaging/ with no transport dependency. MassTransitMessagePublisher.cs in Pennies.Messaging.RabbitMQ provides the concrete RabbitMQ/MassTransit implementation, and Pennies.Worker runs the background consumers: SendConfirmationEmailConsumer handles email delivery via SMTP, and ExpenseCreatedConsumer logs the event.
tour.languageLesson: MassTransit consumers implement IConsumer<TMessage>. The framework handles queue creation, message serialisation, and retry policies. Separating the contract library (Pennies.Messaging) from the transport adapter (Pennies.Messaging.RabbitMQ) means the publisher code never references RabbitMQ directly — the transport can be swapped without touching business logic.

tour.title: Dashboard & Expense UI
tour.description: The authenticated pages (dashboard and expense list/add) bring together every layer explored so far. web/src/routes/_authenticated/dashboard.tsx fetches the expense summary via TanStack Query and renders it through the desktop Dashboard component (SummaryCards + RecentList). The expenses list route supports filter and sort via URL search params, feeding the desktop ExpenseList and mobile ExpenseList components. web/src/lib/pennies.ts provides the shared category-to-label maps, date helpers, and VND currency formatter used across all expense UI.
